metadata.description: Four Claude Code plugins that keep your projects organized, safe, and easy to work in — automatically, with almost no setup.

plugins.name: task-queue
plugins.description: Turns your requests into a live to-do list you can watch Claude work through, and remembers unfinished work between sessions. Step away and it keeps going on its own — handling the routine work and setting the big decisions aside for you to review when you're back. One command ships finished work as a pull request.

plugins.name: tidy
plugins.description: Keeps your code clean and safe while you work: it auto-formats the file you just edited, shows what else your change might affect, and runs your project's own tests before treating anything as done — so mistakes get caught early. Ask it for a full-project cleanup any time. Tests stay optional; nothing is forced on you.

plugins.name: charter
plugins.description: Helps Claude understand your project before it starts changing things. It points out missing documentation, spots the files that keep breaking (from your git history), and checks finished work against the decisions you've written down — so changes stay true to how you want the project built.

plugins.name: hud
plugins.description: One clean status line that shows everything at a glance: whether your tests are passing, whether the safety checks are on, which project and branch you're in, and anything waiting on your decision. Updates live and costs nothing in tokens.
